Document:
# Welcome to OpenEdge 14 Development
OpenEdge 14 is a medieval fantasy sandbox RPG built on the [bold]Space Station 14[/bold] engine and powered by [bold]RobustToolbox[/bold]. This section is a comprehensive guide for developers working on the project.
Whether you're adding new features, fixing bugs, or understanding how the game works, you'll find detailed documentation here about architecture, systems, and implementation patterns.
## What You'll Find Here
### Architecture & Design
[textlink="Architecture & Design" link="OE14_DEV_Architecture"] — Learn how OpenEdge 14 is built: - [textlink="Entity-Component-System" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_ECS"] (ECS) — the core architecture pattern - [textlink="IoC Container" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_IoC"] — dependency injection and service resolution - [textlink="Network Synchronization" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_Netcode"] — how client and server stay in sync - [textlink="Event System" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_Events"] — how systems communicate - [textlink="Modifier System" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_Modifiers"] — stat and effect modifiers
### Character Stats System
[textlink="Character Stats System" link="OE14_DEV_CharacterStats"] — Deep dive into how character stats work: - [textlink="Stat Formulas & Calculations" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Formulas"] — the math behind stats - [textlink="Stat Modifiers & Overflow" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Modifiers"] — how modifiers stack and overflow - [textlink="Spell Fallback System" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Fallback"] — how spells interact with stat limits - [textlink="Network Implementation" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Network"] — how stats sync to client - [textlink="Code Examples" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Code"] — actual implementations
### Skill & Spell System
[textlink="Skill & Spell System" link="OE14_DEV_Skills"] — Understand how spells and skills work: - [textlink="Skill Trees & Distribution" link="OE14_DEV_Skills_Trees"] — point allocation and progression - [textlink="Spell Mechanics" link="OE14_DEV_Skills_Mechanics"] — casting, effects, and resolution - [textlink="Mana System" link="OE14_DEV_Skills_Mana"] — mana pools and regeneration - [textlink="Creating Custom Skills" link="OE14_DEV_Skills_Custom"] — how to add new skills
### Game Flow
[textlink="Game Flow" link="OE14_DEV_GameFlow"] — Learn the lifecycle of a round and player: - [textlink="Round Flow & States" link="OE14_DEV_Flow_Rounds"] — PreRoundLobby, InRound, PostRound - [textlink="Player Spawn & Loading" link="OE14_DEV_Flow_Spawn"] — character creation and initialization - [textlink="Admin Tools & Commands" link="OE14_DEV_Flow_Admin"] — admin system and tooling - [textlink="Event Flow & Lifecycle" link="OE14_DEV_Flow_Events"] — entity and system lifecycle
### System Reference
[textlink="System Reference" link="OE14_DEV_SystemReference"] — Quick reference for major systems: - [textlink="OE14CharacterStats System" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Stats"] - [textlink="Magic & Spell Systems" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Magic"] - [textlink="Crafting Systems" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Crafting"] - [textlink="Economy System" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Economy"] - [textlink="Combat System" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Combat"] - [textlink="Other Systems" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Other"]
### Development Guides
[textlink="Development Guides" link="OE14_DEV_Guides"] — Step-by-step guides for common tasks: - [textlink="Adding New Skills" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Skills"] - [textlink="Adding New Spells" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Spells"] - [textlink="Adding New Jobs" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Jobs"] - [textlink="Adding New Species" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Species"] - [textlink="Database Migrations" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Migrations"] - [textlink="Testing & Debugging" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Testing"]
## Quick Start for New Developers
1. [bold]Set up your environment[/bold] — see CLAUDE.md in the repository root 2. [bold]Read Architecture & Design[/bold] — [textlink="Architecture & Design" link="OE14_DEV_Architecture"] — understand ECS and how the project is structured 3. [bold]Pick a system[/bold] — [textlink="System Reference" link="OE14_DEV_SystemReference"] has documentation for each major system 4. [bold]Follow a guide[/bold] — [textlink="Development Guides" link="OE14_DEV_Guides"] has step-by-step instructions for common tasks 5. [bold]Reference the code[/bold] — all pages include code examples and file paths
## Common Tasks
[bold]I want to add a new spell...[/bold] Read [textlink="Adding New Spells" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Spells"] and [textlink="Spell Mechanics" link="OE14_DEV_Skills_Mechanics"]
[bold]I want to understand why my stat modifier is being capped...[/bold] Read [textlink="Spell Fallback System" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Fallback"] and [textlink="Stat Modifiers & Overflow" link="OE14_DEV_Stats_Modifiers"]
[bold]I want to sync data between server and client...[/bold] Read [textlink="Network Synchronization" link="OE14_DEV_Arch_Netcode"]
[bold]I want to add a new job...[/bold] Read [textlink="Adding New Jobs" link="OE14_DEV_Guide_Jobs"]
[bold]I need to fix a bug in the economy system...[/bold] Read [textlink="Economy System" link="OE14_DEV_SysRef_Economy"]
## How This Guidebook is Structured
Each page is designed to be: - [bold]Self-contained[/bold] — can read in any order - [bold]Practical[/bold] — includes code examples and file paths - [bold]Linked[/bold] — references to related pages - [bold]Accessible[/bold] — written for developers of all experience levels (though assumes C# and .NET knowledge)
## Design Philosophy
OpenEdge 14 follows these principles:
[bold]Entity-Component-System[/bold] — every "thing" in the world is an Entity with Components, processed by Systems
[bold]Event-Driven[/bold] — systems communicate through events, not direct calls
[bold]Network-First[/bold] — all significant state is synchronized between server and client
[bold]Modifier-Based[/bold] — temporary changes (stat bonuses, effects) use a modifier system, not direct mutation
[bold]YAML Configuration[/bold] — game content is defined in human-readable YAML, not hardcoded
Understanding these principles will make the codebase much easier to navigate.
## Performance Notes
- The game runs at 60 ticks per second - Network updates are throttled and batched - Systems iterate over matching entities efficiently - Avoid allocating new objects in hot loops - Use object pooling for frequently created/destroyed entities
## Contributing
When adding new systems or features: 1. [bold]Follow existing patterns[/bold] — if similar systems exist, match their structure 2. [bold]Document your work[/bold] — leave comments for complex logic 3. [bold]Test thoroughly[/bold] — both locally and with other developers 4. [bold]Update this guidebook[/bold] — add pages or examples if you create new systems
## External References
Since OpenEdge 14 is based on Space Station 14, some concepts come from upstream:
- [bold]RobustToolbox[/bold] — core engine (SS14 documentation) - [bold]Space Station 14[/bold] — base game framework (github.com/space-wizards/space-station-14) - [bold].NET 9[/bold] — programming language and runtime
This guidebook focuses on OpenEdge-specific implementations.
## Questions?
- Check the [bold]System Reference[/bold] for your specific system - Look at [bold]Development Guides[/bold] for step-by-step instructions - Read [bold]Architecture & Design[/bold] if you need fundamental concepts - Check CLAUDE.md in the repository root for project conventions
Good luck, and welcome to development!
